title: ZCBOR_STATE_E backup count of 1 may be insufficient for rollback
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Line: 280

CATEGORY: API MISUSE

The ZCBOR_STATE_E macro is initialized with backup_num=1:
  ZCBOR_STATE_E(state, 1, buf, buflen, 1);

zcbor backup states are used for rollback when encoding fails mid-operation. With nested structures (list containing maps), a single backup may be insufficient.

If encoding fails after starting a map but before completing it, and zcbor needs to rollback past the list start, it may not have enough backup states.

The encoding structure is:
  zcbor_list_start_encode  (level 1)
    zcbor_map_start_encode (level 2)
      ... encode fields ...
    zcbor_map_end_encode
  zcbor_list_end_encode

With backup_num=1, a failure at level 2 that needs to rollback to before level 1 could fail.

IMPACT:
- Potential zcbor assertion failure or undefined behavior on encode error
- Error recovery may leave CBOR state corrupted
- Difficult to debug intermittent failures

FIX: Increase backup_num to match nesting depth (2 or more):
  ZCBOR_STATE_E(state, 2, buf, buflen, 1);

Note: This depends on zcbor version and exact rollback requirements. Review zcbor documentation for correct backup_num calculation.
